title: Capture real token usage and cost from stream-json result event
source: Anthropic docs: Claude Code CLI reference (stream-json output format) — https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/cli-reference
description: delegate.py estimates cost with a crude 4-chars-per-token heuristic (CostTracker.estimate, lines 156-204), ignoring the authoritative usage data the CLI already emits. In stream mode the 'result' event (parsed at delegate.py:1900) carries a 'usage' object (input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens) and a 'total_cost_usd' field. Using the real numbers makes budget gating and the Prometheus ledger accurate instead of guessed, and surfaces prompt-cache savings.
proposed_changes: In the stream-json loop, when etype=='result', read event.get('usage', {}) and event.get('total_cost_usd'). Add a CostTracker.record_actual(ticket_id, agent, usage, total_cost_usd, model) that stores real input/output/cache_read/cache_creation tokens and the CLI-reported cost. Prefer actual usage when present and fall back to the char-based estimate() only for non-stream runs. Update the cost line at delegate.py:2216 to display cache_read tokens and a '(cached)' marker when cache_read_input_tokens > 0.
